introSection: Raging Wolf Bobs was a wooden roller coaster located at Geauga Lake & Wildwater Kingdom in Aurora, Ohio, USA. It was constructed by the Dinn Corporation and opened to the public in May 1988.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Raging Wolf Bobs opened on May 28, 1988. It originally had a natural color. The structure was later painted white.

The ride operated until June 16, 2007, following an incident. Later that season, park owners Cedar Fair announced the permanent closure of Geauga Lake, sealing the fate of Raging Wolf Bobs.

The ride remained standing but not operating for several years after the park's closure. It was removed sometime between April 2012 and June 2014.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Raging Wolf Bobs was based on the Bobs at Riverview Park in Chicago, Illinois. Raging Wolf Bobs had a max height of, a drop of and a max speed of.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The Raging Wolf Bobs' original trailered PTC trains were replaced by a Gerstlauer train taken from the Villain for the 2003 season. For the 2005 season, another used Gerstlauer train from Holiday World's Legend was added.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On June 16, 2007, a train derailed after it rolled backward on one of the hills. The ride did not reopen.

fullText:
Raging Wolf Bobs was a wooden roller coaster located at Geauga Lake & Wildwater Kingdom in Aurora, Ohio, USA. It was constructed by the Dinn Corporation and opened to the public in May 1988.

Raging Wolf Bobs opened on May 28, 1988. It originally had a natural color. The structure was later painted white.

The ride operated until June 16, 2007, following an incident. Later that season, park owners Cedar Fair announced the permanent closure of Geauga Lake, sealing the fate of Raging Wolf Bobs.

The ride remained standing but not operating for several years after the park's closure. It was removed sometime between April 2012 and June 2014.

Raging Wolf Bobs was based on the Bobs at Riverview Park in Chicago, Illinois. Raging Wolf Bobs had a max height of, a drop of and a max speed of.

The Raging Wolf Bobs' original trailered PTC trains were replaced by a Gerstlauer train taken from the Villain for the 2003 season. For the 2005 season, another used Gerstlauer train from Holiday World's Legend was added.

On June 16, 2007, a train derailed after it rolled backward on one of the hills. The ride did not reopen.
